questions.stem: An ac-dc servo system is given with: KA=5 Vdc/Vdc, Ks=7 Vrms/rad, Kd=3 Vdc/Vrms, KT=1 Nm/A, Kb=1 V/rad/s, Ra=1 Ω, JL=5 kg.m², BL=2.5 Nm/rad/s, Jm=1 kg.m², Bm=0.55 Nm/rad/s, gear ratio M/L=10, M/s=1/5. Draw the block diagram.
common_errors: Incorrect reflected inertia/friction calculation through gear train
Missing demodulator or synchro pair blocks
Wrong gear ratio direction (M/L vs L/M)
questions.solution_method: Identify each component (synchro pair, demodulator, amplifier, DC motor with armature circuit, gear train, load). Reflect JL and BL to motor shaft using n²=1/100, compute Jeq and Beq. Draw forward path from θR through Ks, Kd, KA, motor TF to θM, then gear ratio to θL. Back-emf provides inner feedback.
key_formulas: Motor TF: θM(s)/Va(s) = KT / [s(RaJeq·s + RaBeq + KbKT)]

questions.stem: For the ac-dc servo system in Q1, determine the closed-loop transfer function θL(s)/θR(s).
common_errors: Forgetting the gear ratio factor in the output (θL = θM/10)
Incorrect loop gain due to unit confusion (Vrms vs Vdc)
questions.solution_method: Compute overall open-loop gain K_open = Ks·Kd·KA·KT / (Ra·Jeq). Apply standard closed-loop formula: θL/θR = (n·G(s))/(1+G(s)·H(s)) where G(s) is motor TF and H(s)=Kb·s (back-emf feedback), then include synchro/demodulator/amplifier chain.

questions.stem: For the ac-dc servo system in Q1 with computed TF, determine the rise time.
common_errors: Using wrong formula for tr when damping ratio is not standard
Confusing rise time definition (10-90% vs 0-100%)
questions.solution_method: From computed closed-loop TF identify ωn and ζ. Apply tr = (π - φ)/(ωd) where φ = arctan(ωd/σ) and ωd = ωn√(1-ζ²).

questions.stem: Sketch the Nyquist plot for G(s) = K(s+1) / [s²(s+2)], choosing the appropriate Nyquist contour. Determine the range of K (K>0) for which the closed-loop system is stable. Verify using Routh stability criterion.
common_errors: Incorrect indentation around double pole at origin — missing two semicircle contributions
Wrong encirclement count due to sign error in N = Z - P
Not checking behavior at s→∞ or on imaginary axis segments
questions.solution_method: G(s) has double pole at origin, so indent Nyquist contour with small semicircle. Evaluate G(jω) for ω: 0⁺ to ∞ (real axis crossing), map small semicircle (two 180° rotations → angle -360°), and the mirror image. Find real-axis crossings of G(jω) by setting Im[G(jω)]=0 and compute Re at those ω. Apply N=Z-P; P=0 (no open-loop RHP poles), so Z=N encirclements → stability condition. Verify by forming CE 1+G(s)=0, expanding and applying RH criterion.
key_formulas: Characteristic equation: s³ + 2s² + Ks + K = 0 (after clearing)

questions.stem: Unity feedback system with forward path K·[1/((s+a)(s+b))]. Step response: peak overshoot = 16.3%, final value = 1. Ramp steady-state error = 0.0625. Find K, a, b (all ≥ 0).
common_errors: Conflating type number with coefficient K/ab for Kv
Not using Mp formula correctly to extract ζ
Assuming a=0 or b=0 without checking type
questions.solution_method: From Mp=16.3% → ζ≈0.5. Final value=1 → DC gain = K/(ab) = 1 (unity). Type-1 system needed for finite ramp error → one of a,b=0; but final value=1 with step means DC gain=1 so actually type-0. Ramp SSE=0.0625 → Kv=16 → K/(a+b)... Re-examine: system is type-0 so ramp error→∞ unless it's type-1. Resolve by setting one pole at origin: if b=0, Kv=K/a=16 and ωn²=K/Jeq, 2ζωn=a+b. Use Mp→ζ, then ωn²=K/a·a=K, 2ζωn=a, Kv=K/a=16.
key_formulas: Kv = lim s→0 [s·G(s)] = K/(a) if b=0

questions.stem: With K, a, b as found in Q3(a), add an integral controller (gain K1) to eliminate ramp steady-state error. Find the range of K1 for closed-loop stability.
common_errors: Adding proportional instead of integral action
Forgetting to rewrite CE after adding integrator
questions.solution_method: Add integrator K1/s in forward path. New open-loop TF = K·K1 / [s(s+a)(s+b)]. Form CE, apply RH criterion to find K1 range that keeps all roots in LHP.

questions.stem: Draw the root contours for the system in Q3 for two values of K (8 and 13) with 0 < K1 < ∞, keeping a and b at computed values.
common_errors: Confusing root locus (K varying) with root contour (K1 varying at fixed K)
Wrong open-loop poles/zeros when K1 is the variable
questions.solution_method: Rearrange CE in the form 1 + K1·[K / (s(s+a)(s+b) + K·0)] = 0 → treat K1 as the variable gain. Identify equivalent open-loop poles (roots of s(s+a)(s+b)=0 for each fixed K) and zeros. Sketch RL rules: n=3 poles, m=0 zeros, 3 asymptotes at 60°,180°,300°. Repeat for K=8 and K=13.

questions.stem: A system is described by: y = 2x - 4z - 5u; z = 3y - 3w + dz/dt; w = 2z; u = w + 2·dz/dt. Draw the SFG (without solving equations) and determine U(s)/X(s) using Mason's Gain Formula.
common_errors: Missing self-loop from dz/dt term (1/s integration branch)
Incorrect loop identification — missing touching/non-touching pairs
Wrong sign on feedback branches
questions.solution_method: Take Laplace of each equation: Y=2X-4Z-5U; Z=3Y-3W+sZ (→Z(1-s)=3Y-3W, rewrite as Z=(3Y-3W)/(1-s)); W=2Z; U=W+2sZ. Assign nodes X,Y,Z,W,U. Draw branches per equation term. Apply Mason's: identify all forward paths X→...→U, compute Δ (graph determinant with all loops), compute Δk (sub-determinants), TF=ΣPkΔk/Δ.

questions.stem: Using the TF U(s)/X(s) from Q4(a) as an open-loop TF for unity negative feedback, draw the polar plot and calculate the Gain Margin and Phase Margin.
common_errors: Not finding phase-crossover frequency correctly
Wrong sign for phase angle (checking +180 vs -180)
questions.solution_method: Substitute s=jω in the TF. Find phase-crossover frequency ωpc where ∠G(jωpc)=-180°. Compute GM=1/|G(jωpc)|. Find gain-crossover frequency ωgc where |G(jωgc)|=1. Compute PM=180°+∠G(jωgc).

questions.stem: Open-loop TF: K / [s²(0.4s + 2)]. Design a phase-lead compensator (with amplifier) using asymptotic Bode plots to meet: Ka = 5, Phase Margin = 10° (take safety factor = 10°). Also draw Bode plots for the compensated system.
common_errors: Using wrong error constant formula for type-2 system (Ka = lim s²G(s))
Not accounting for phase added by compensator at new ωgc
Forgetting to include amplifier gain to restore Ka after compensation
Placing φmax at wrong frequency
questions.solution_method: Step 1: Ka=lim s→0 [s²·G_compensated(s)]=5 → set K. Step 2: Plot uncompensated Bode, find ωgc and PM. Step 3: Required additional phase = PMdesired + margin - PM_uncompensated. Step 4: Compute α=(1-sinφmax)/(1+sinφmax), compensator zero at 1/τ, pole at 1/(ατ). Step 5: Place ωmax at new ωgc where |G(jω)|=−10log(1/α) dB. Step 6: Write Gc(s)=(1+αTs)/(1+Ts)/α, choose α and T. Verify PM of compensated system.
key_formulas: Ka = K/2 for G(s)=K/[s²(0.4s+2)] → K=10 for Ka=5
Gain at ωmax from uncompensated = -10log(1/α) dB

notes: First Semester 2009-10 paper (Dec 2009). Course code AAOC C321 (not F242) — classify as external reference paper for typology. Total 120 marks. Q5 is 30 marks and requires semi-log graph paper — heavy Bode design. Q2 combines Nyquist + RH verification (25 marks). Q3 is a multi-part synthesis problem spanning time-domain specs, error constants, root contour.
